overrides.override_summary: Prospective franchisees encouraged to consult counsel on applicability of California and federal laws to provisions restricting venue outside California
overrides.why_it_matters: California law may override Illinois arbitration venue requirement

overrides.override_summary: Illinois governing law provision might not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: California franchisees may be able to apply California law instead of Illinois law

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages clause may be unenforceable under California Civil Code Section 1671
overrides.why_it_matters: Reduces franchisee exposure to liquidated damages upon termination

overrides.override_summary: Release upon renewal/transfer cannot waive compliance with Franchise Investment Law; anti-fraud/anti-waiver protections under Corp. Code §§31512, 31512.1
overrides.why_it_matters: California franchisees retain right to pursue state law claims even after signing release

overrides.override_summary: Spousal liability warning — community property state means both spouses' assets at risk
overrides.why_it_matters: Personal financial risk extends to spouse's assets

overrides.override_summary: No waiver/disclaimer of reliance provision applies
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects franchisee from signing away fraud-in-inducement claims

overrides.override_summary: Release upon renewal/transfer does not apply to Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law claims
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland franchisees retain state law protections upon renewal or transfer

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may bring action in Maryland for claims under Maryland law (subject to arbitration)
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides Illinois forum requirement for Maryland law claims

overrides.override_summary: To extent required by Maryland law, franchisee may bring action in Maryland
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland law may apply instead of Illinois law for Maryland Franchise Law claims

overrides.override_summary: Termination upon insolvency might not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law
overrides.why_it_matters: Insolvency alone may not trigger termination

overrides.override_summary: Cannot terminate prior to expiration except for good cause (30-day cure); cannot refuse to renew without fair market value compensation
overrides.why_it_matters: Strongest franchisee termination protections; franchisor must pay fair market value on non-renewal if term <5 years and post-term noncompete exists or <6 months notice

overrides.override_summary: Provision requiring arbitration/litigation outside Michigan is void; HOWEVER franchisor states it will enforce arbitration provisions despite this law
overrides.why_it_matters: Active conflict between state law and franchisor position — may require litigation to resolve

overrides.override_summary: 90 days' notice of termination with 60 days to cure; 180 days' notice for non-renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: Longer notice and cure periods than the FA provides

overrides.override_summary: Litigation cannot be conducted outside Minnesota; franchisee retains right to submit matters to Minnesota courts (subject to arbitration)
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides Illinois forum for Minnesota franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee cannot waive rights to jury trial, liquidated damages, termination penalties, or judgment notes (to extent prohibited by Minn. Rule 2860.4400J)
overrides.why_it_matters: Liquidated damages provisions may be unenforceable; jury trial rights preserved

overrides.override_summary: Release upon renewal/transfer does not apply to claims under Minn. Rule 2860.4400D
overrides.why_it_matters: Minnesota franchisees retain state law claims even upon renewal/transfer

overrides.override_summary: Commissioner determined termination/liquidated damages to be unfair, unjust and inequitable; parties agree to enforce to extent law allows
overrides.why_it_matters: Liquidated damages may be reduced or voided by ND Commissioner

overrides.override_summary: Releases do not apply to claims arising under North Dakota Franchise Investment Law
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees retain state law claims

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration venue may be modified to mutually agreeable site (to extent required by ND law, unless preempted by FAA)
overrides.why_it_matters: May avoid Chicago arbitration venue

overrides.override_summary: To extent required by law, North Dakota law applies
overrides.why_it_matters: ND law may override Illinois governing law

overrides.override_summary: Provision restricting venue outside RI is void with respect to Rhode Island Franchise Investment Act claims
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchisees can litigate state law claims in RI

overrides.override_summary: Illinois law applies except as required for claims under RI Franchise Investment Act
overrides.why_it_matters: RI law controls for state franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Unlawful to cancel franchise without reasonable cause per Virginia Retail Franchising Act §13.1-564
overrides.why_it_matters: Non-curable default grounds that don't constitute 'reasonable cause' may be unenforceable
